rulings.Case Title: PRATT, AVIS INDIVIDUALLY AND AS SUCCESSOR-IN-INTER et al VS. AC AND S, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Hearing Re Specially Appearing Defendant Patriarch Partners Llc'S Motion To Quash Service Of Summons On Second Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, September 4, 2014 in Department 503, Line 1. Specially appearing defendant Patriarch Partners LLC's motion to quash service of summons: hearing required. The hearing will be at 9:45a.m. Both sides must bring proposed orders to the hearing. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: PETER FIMBRES VS. ASBESTOS CORPORATION LIMITED et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Judgment On Pleadings By Defendant Rpi Company (Doe 1001)
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, September 4, 2014 in Department 503, Line 4. Defendant RPI Company's motion for judgment on the pleadings is off calendar at the moving party's request. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: RICHARD MCLAIN et al VS. CRANE CO. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To File First Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, September 4, 2014 in Department 503, Line 2. Plaintiff's motion for leave to file first amended complaint is granted. No opposition filed. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: JOHN WILLIAM THOMPSON et al VS. 3M COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT / Notice Of Motion For Summary Judgment Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication Of Plaintiffs Causes Of Action For Strict Liability, False Representation, And Their Claim For Punitive Damages; Proof Of Service
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, September 4, 2014 in Department 503, Line 3. Defendant J.T. Thorpe & Son, Inc.'s motion for summary judgment or, in the alternative, summary adjudication is off calendar at the moving party's request. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: JOHN WILLIAM THOMPSON et al VS. 3M COMPANY et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, September 4, 2014 in Department 503, Line 3. Defendant ProBuild Company, LLC's motion for summary judgment is denied. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that it has no liability for injury caused by products purchased and/or used by Mr. Thompson from the Lumbermen's store in Forest Grove, Oregon between 1970 and 1974. Alternatively, at a minimum the Agreement of Purchase creates a triable issue of fact for the jury. Plaintiffs' objections to Alvin Buller's declaration are overruled. Plaintiffs' objections to Maury Cuje's declaration are overruled as to paragraphs 9, 10, 11 and 12 and sustained as to paragraph 13. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)
